  "text": "The Grafana dashboard JSON schema version migration pipeline is implemented in two mirroring layers: a Go backend and a TypeScript frontend.\n\n**Backend (Go) — apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/**\n\n1. **Migration runner** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`): The top-level entry point. It exposes three public functions: `Initialize(dsInfoProvider)` (called once at startup to populate the migration map via `GetMigrations`), `Migrate(ctx, dash, targetVersion)` (the main migration function that steps through each schema version), and `ResetForTesting()`. Internally it uses a singleton `migrator` struct with a ready-channel for thread-safe lazy initialization.\n\n2. **Versioned migration function registry** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go`): Contains `GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider DataSourceInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` which returns a map from schema version integer (2–42) to the corresponding migration function. It also defines the `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` type (`func(context.Context, map[string]interface{}) error`), the `DataSourceInfoProvider` interface, `DataSourceInfo` struct, `GetSchemaVersion`, `MIN_VERSION=0`, and `LATEST_VERSION=42`.\n\n3. **Individual migration steps** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go` through `v42.go`): Each file implements a single versioned migration. For example:\n   - `V2` (v2.go): migrates legacy `services.filter` structure to top-level `time` and `templating`.\n   - `V36` (v36.go): converts string datasource references to structured UID-based objects; it is a higher-order function that takes a `DataSourceInfoProvider` and returns a `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc`.\n   Other notable factory functions that take `dsInfoProvider` are `V33` and `V36`.\n\n4. **Utility/support files**: `migration_utils.go`, `datasource_utils.go`, `errors.go` (in schemaversion), and `frontend_defaults.go` (in migration) which applies DashboardModel/PanelModel constructor defaults to replicate frontend behavior before running the migration pipeline.\n\n5. **API-version conversion** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/`): `conversion.go` registers Kubernetes-style runtime conversions between dashboard API versions (v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1) via `RegisterConversions`. Individual conversion logic lives in `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`, and `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`.\n\n**Frontend (TypeScript) — public/app/features/dashboard/state/**\n\n`DashboardMigrator.ts` contains the `DashboardMigrator` class and the `DASHBOARD_SCHEMA_VERSION = 42` constant. The class mirrors the backend migration logic and is invoked by `DashboardModel.updateSchema()`. It iterates from the dashboard's current schema version up to the latest, applying the same per-version migration logic. The frontend is the historical origin; the Go backend was created to replicate it exactly, allowing backend-side migration before the frontend ever sees the JSON."
}

  "text": "The Grafana dashboard JSON schema version migration pipeline is implemented in two mirroring layers: a Go backend and a TypeScript frontend.\n\n**Backend (Go) — apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/**\n\n1. **Migration runner** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`): The top-level entry point. It exposes three public functions: `Initialize(dsInfoProvider)` (called once at startup to populate the migration map via `GetMigrations`), `Migrate(ctx, dash, targetVersion)` (the main migration function that steps through each schema version), and `ResetForTesting()`. Internally it uses a singleton `migrator` struct with a ready-channel for thread-safe lazy initialization.\n\n2. **Versioned migration function registry** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go`): Contains `GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider DataSourceInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` which returns a map from schema version integer (2–42) to the corresponding migration function. It also defines the `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` type (`func(context.Context, map[string]interface{}) error`), the `DataSourceInfoProvider` interface, `DataSourceInfo` struct, `GetSchemaVersion`, `MIN_VERSION=0`, and `LATEST_VERSION=42`.\n\n3. **Individual migration steps** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go` through `v42.go`): Each file implements a single versioned migration. For example:\n   - `V2` (v2.go): migrates legacy `services.filter` structure to top-level `time` and `templating`.\n   - `V36` (v36.go): converts string datasource references to structured UID-based objects; it is a higher-order function that takes a `DataSourceInfoProvider` and returns a `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc`.\n   Other notable factory functions that take `dsInfoProvider` are `V33` and `V36`.\n\n4. **Utility/support files**: `migration_utils.go`, `datasource_utils.go`, `errors.go` (in schemaversion), and `frontend_defaults.go` (in migration) which applies DashboardModel/PanelModel constructor defaults to replicate frontend behavior before running the migration pipeline.\n\n5. **API-version conversion** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/`): `conversion.go` registers Kubernetes-style runtime conversions between dashboard API versions (v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1) via `RegisterConversions`. Individual conversion logic lives in `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`, and `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`.\n\n**Frontend (TypeScript) — public/app/features/dashboard/state/**\n\n`DashboardMigrator.ts` contains the `DashboardMigrator` class and the `DASHBOARD_SCHEMA_VERSION = 42` constant. The class mirrors the backend migration logic and is invoked by `DashboardModel.updateSchema()`. It iterates from the dashboard's current schema version up to the latest, applying the same per-version migration logic. The frontend is the historical origin; the Go backend was created to replicate it exactly, allowing backend-side migration before the frontend ever sees the JSON."
}

  "text": "The Grafana dashboard JSON schema version migration pipeline is implemented in two mirroring layers: a Go backend and a TypeScript frontend.\n\n**Backend (Go) — apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/**\n\n1. **Migration runner** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`): The top-level entry point. It exposes three public functions: `Initialize(dsInfoProvider)` (called once at startup to populate the migration map via `GetMigrations`), `Migrate(ctx, dash, targetVersion)` (the main migration function that steps through each schema version), and `ResetForTesting()`. Internally it uses a singleton `migrator` struct with a ready-channel for thread-safe lazy initialization.\n\n2. **Versioned migration function registry** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go`): Contains `GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider DataSourceInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` which returns a map from schema version integer (2–42) to the corresponding migration function. It also defines the `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` type (`func(context.Context, map[string]interface{}) error`), the `DataSourceInfoProvider` interface, `DataSourceInfo` struct, `GetSchemaVersion`, `MIN_VERSION=0`, and `LATEST_VERSION=42`.\n\n3. **Individual migration steps** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go` through `v42.go`): Each file implements a single versioned migration. For example:\n   - `V2` (v2.go): migrates legacy `services.filter` structure to top-level `time` and `templating`.\n   - `V36` (v36.go): converts string datasource references to structured UID-based objects; it is a higher-order function that takes a `DataSourceInfoProvider` and returns a `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc`.\n   Other notable factory functions that take `dsInfoProvider` are `V33` and `V36`.\n\n4. **Utility/support files**: `migration_utils.go`, `datasource_utils.go`, `errors.go` (in schemaversion), and `frontend_defaults.go` (in migration) which applies DashboardModel/PanelModel constructor defaults to replicate frontend behavior before running the migration pipeline.\n\n5. **API-version conversion** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/`): `conversion.go` registers Kubernetes-style runtime conversions between dashboard API versions (v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1) via `RegisterConversions`. Individual conversion logic lives in `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`, and `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`.\n\n**Frontend (TypeScript) — public/app/features/dashboard/state/**\n\n`DashboardMigrator.ts` contains the `DashboardMigrator` class and the `DASHBOARD_SCHEMA_VERSION = 42` constant. The class mirrors the backend migration logic and is invoked by `DashboardModel.updateSchema()`. It iterates from the dashboard's current schema version up to the latest, applying the same per-version migration logic. The frontend is the historical origin; the Go backend was created to replicate it exactly, allowing backend-side migration before the frontend ever sees the JSON."

  "text": "The Grafana dashboard JSON schema version migration pipeline is implemented in two mirroring layers: a Go backend and a TypeScript frontend.\n\n**Backend (Go) — apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/**\n\n1. **Migration runner** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`): The top-level entry point. It exposes three public functions: `Initialize(dsInfoProvider)` (called once at startup to populate the migration map via `GetMigrations`), `Migrate(ctx, dash, targetVersion)` (the main migration function that iterates nextVersion from inputVersion+1 to targetVersion calling the registered per-version func), and `ResetForTesting()`. Internally uses a singleton `migrator` struct with a ready-channel for thread-safe lazy initialization.\n\n2. **Versioned migration function registry** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go`): Contains `GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider DataSourceInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` returning a map from schema version integer (2–42) to the corresponding migration function. Also defines the `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` type (`func(context.Context, map[string]interface{}) error`), the `DataSourceInfoProvider` interface, `DataSourceInfo` struct, `GetSchemaVersion`, `MIN_VERSION=0`, and `LATEST_VERSION=42`.\n\n3. **Individual migration steps** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go` through `v42.go`): Each file implements one versioned migration. Examples: `V2` migrates the legacy `services.filter` structure; `V36` (a factory taking `DataSourceInfoProvider`) converts string datasource references to structured UID-based objects. `V33` is another factory function.\n\n4. **Frontend defaults replication** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go`): Applies DashboardModel/PanelModel constructor defaults before running the schema migration pipeline, exactly replicating frontend constructor behavior.\n\n5. **Utility/support** (`schemaversion/migration_utils.go`, `schemaversion/datasource_utils.go`, `schemaversion/errors.go`).\n\n6. **API-version conversion** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/`): `conversion.go` registers Kubernetes-style runtime conversions between dashboard API versions (v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1) via `RegisterConversions`. Individual conversion logic lives in `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`, and `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`.\n\n**Frontend (TypeScript) — public/app/features/dashboard/state/**\n\n`DashboardMigrator.ts` contains the `DashboardMigrator` class and the `DASHBOARD_SCHEMA_VERSION = 42` constant. This class is the historical origin of the migration logic and is invoked by `DashboardModel.updateSchema()`. The Go backend was created to replicate it exactly for server-side migration."
}

  "text": "The Grafana dashboard JSON schema version migration pipeline is implemented in two mirroring layers: a Go backend and a TypeScript frontend.\n\n**Backend (Go) — apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/**\n\n1. **Migration runner** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go`): The top-level entry point. It exposes three public functions: `Initialize(dsInfoProvider)` (called once at startup to populate the migration map via `GetMigrations`), `Migrate(ctx, dash, targetVersion)` (the main migration function that iterates nextVersion from inputVersion+1 to targetVersion calling the registered per-version func), and `ResetForTesting()`. Internally uses a singleton `migrator` struct with a ready-channel for thread-safe lazy initialization.\n\n2. **Versioned migration function registry** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go`): Contains `GetMigrations(dsInfoProvider DataSourceInfoProvider) map[int]SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` returning a map from schema version integer (2–42) to the corresponding migration function. Also defines the `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` type (`func(context.Context, map[string]interface{}) error`), the `DataSourceInfoProvider` interface, `DataSourceInfo` struct, `GetSchemaVersion`, `MIN_VERSION=0`, and `LATEST_VERSION=42`.\n\n3. **Individual migration steps** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v2.go` through `v42.go`): Each file implements one versioned migration. Examples: `V2` migrates the legacy `services.filter` structure; `V36` (a factory taking `DataSourceInfoProvider`) converts string datasource references to structured UID-based objects. `V33` is another factory function.\n\n4. **Frontend defaults replication** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go`): Applies DashboardModel/PanelModel constructor defaults before running the schema migration pipeline, exactly replicating frontend constructor behavior.\n\n5. **Utility/support** (`schemaversion/migration_utils.go`, `schemaversion/datasource_utils.go`, `schemaversion/errors.go`).\n\n6. **API-version conversion** (`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/`): `conversion.go` registers Kubernetes-style runtime conversions between dashboard API versions (v0alpha1, v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1) via `RegisterConversions`. Individual conversion logic lives in `v0.go`, `v1.go`, `v2.go`, and `v2alpha1_to_v2beta1.go`.\n\n**Frontend (TypeScript) — public/app/features/dashboard/state/**\n\n`DashboardMigrator.ts` contains the `DashboardMigrator` class and the `DASHBOARD_SCHEMA_VERSION = 42` constant. This class is the historical origin of the migration logic and is invoked by `DashboardModel.updateSchema()`. The Go backend was created to replicate it exactly for server-side migration."
